mechanism: The Godley identity (Private + Government + Foreign ≡ 0) constrains the sustainability of the Kalecki profit channel. The private sector surplus of 3-4% GDP depends entirely on the government deficit. Both resolution paths (consolidation → profit reduction, continuation → term premium loading) eventually undermine the Kalecki buffer.
regime_dependence: Holds as accounting identity at all times. The binding constraint emerges when deficit-driven issuance pushes term premium high enough to offset the profit support — timing is unknown but direction is certain.
